subject: Gaming
title: What Makes a Game Indie
description: A quick, practical guide to What Makes a Game Indie.

hook.text: Indie means independent. But some 'indie' games have million-dollar budgets. Where's the real line?

buildup.text: Originally, indie meant self-funded and self-published. No publisher, no corporate oversight, total creative freedom.

discovery.text: The definition blurred when publishers started funding 'indie-style' games. Budget alone doesn't define indie.

twist.text: Some massive studios call their games indie for marketing. The label became a brand, not just a structure.

climax.text: True indie is about creative independence—making the game you want without anyone telling you to change it.

punchline.text: Indie isn't a budget. It's a mindset. Creative freedom defines it.

quiz.question: What truly defines an indie game?
options: Being made by exactly one person
